evals.prompt: Set up a basic Duende IdentityServer host in Program.cs with in-memory stores. Define a single API scope called 'weatherapi' and the standard openid and profile identity resources. Enable all event types for diagnostics. No clients yet — just the resource definitions.
evals.expected_output: Program.cs registers IdentityServer with AddIdentityServer, configures event raising, adds in-memory identity resources (openid + profile), in-memory API scopes (weatherapi), and calls UseIdentityServer in the middleware pipeline.
assertions: Output contains a call to builder.Services.AddIdentityServer with an options lambda
Output enables all four event types: RaiseErrorEvents, RaiseInformationEvents, RaiseFailureEvents, RaiseSuccessEvents all set to true
Output calls AddInMemoryIdentityResources with at least IdentityResources.OpenId() and IdentityResources.Profile()
Output calls AddInMemoryApiScopes with a scope named 'weatherapi'
Output calls app.UseIdentityServer() and does NOT separately call app.UseAuthentication()

evals.prompt: Add a machine-to-machine client called 'batch-processor' that uses client credentials to access two API scopes: 'orders.read' and 'inventory.write'. Also add an interactive web application client called 'admin-portal' that uses authorization code flow with PKCE, supports refresh tokens, and can access the openid, profile, email scopes plus 'orders.read'. The admin portal's redirect URI is https://admin.example.com/signin-oidc and post-logout redirect is https://admin.example.com/signout-callback-oidc.
evals.expected_output: Two client definitions: one M2M client with ClientCredentials grant type and two API scopes, and one interactive client with Code grant type, PKCE, AllowOfflineAccess enabled, proper redirect URIs, and the correct scope list including openid.
assertions: Output defines a client with ClientId 'batch-processor' using GrantTypes.ClientCredentials with AllowedScopes containing 'orders.read' and 'inventory.write'
Output defines a client with ClientId 'admin-portal' using GrantTypes.Code with RequirePkce true (explicit or by noting default)
The admin-portal client has AllowOfflineAccess set to true to enable refresh tokens
The admin-portal client includes IdentityServerConstants.StandardScopes.OpenId (or 'openid') in its AllowedScopes
The admin-portal client has RedirectUris containing 'https://admin.example.com/signin-oidc' and PostLogoutRedirectUris containing 'https://admin.example.com/signout-callback-oidc'

evals.prompt: I have two separate microservices: a 'catalog-api' and an 'orders-api'. Both need a 'read' scope, and catalog-api also needs a 'write' scope. I want tokens issued for catalog-api to not be usable at orders-api and vice versa. How do I configure the API resources and scopes to achieve audience isolation? Show me the full resource and scope configuration.
evals.expected_output: Configuration uses both ApiScope and ApiResource. ApiScopes define 'read' and 'write'. Two ApiResource entries ('catalog-api' and 'orders-api') each list their allowed scopes. The ApiResource controls the 'aud' claim to isolate tokens per API.
assertions: Output defines ApiScope entries for at least 'read' and 'write'
Output defines an ApiResource with name 'catalog-api' that includes both 'read' and 'write' in its Scopes collection
Output defines an ApiResource with name 'orders-api' that includes 'read' in its Scopes collection but not 'write'
Output calls AddInMemoryApiResources to register the API resources with IdentityServer

evals.prompt: Configure automatic key management for a production IdentityServer deployment running behind a load balancer. Keys should rotate every 60 days, be announced 7 days before activation, and retained for 21 days after retirement. Also enable data protection for keys at rest. All instances share a network path at /mnt/shared/identity-keys for key storage.
evals.expected_output: IdentityServerOptions.KeyManagement configured with RotationInterval=60 days, PropagationTime=7 days, RetentionDuration=21 days, DataProtectKeys=true, and KeyPath set to the shared mount path.
assertions: Output sets options.KeyManagement.RotationInterval to TimeSpan.FromDays(60)
Output sets options.KeyManagement.PropagationTime to TimeSpan.FromDays(7)
Output sets options.KeyManagement.RetentionDuration to TimeSpan.FromDays(21)
Output sets options.KeyManagement.DataProtectKeys to true
Output sets options.KeyManagement.KeyPath to '/mnt/shared/identity-keys'

evals.prompt: Enable server-side sessions on my IdentityServer and configure it so that expired sessions trigger back-channel logout notifications to clients. Expired sessions should be cleaned up every 5 minutes. Also coordinate client token lifetimes with the user session so that refresh tokens are revoked when a user's session ends.
evals.expected_output: Configuration calls AddServerSideSessions() on the builder, sets RemoveExpiredSessionsFrequency to 5 minutes, ExpiredSessionsTriggerBackchannelLogout to true, and CoordinateClientLifetimesWithUserSession to true.
assertions: Output calls .AddServerSideSessions() on the IdentityServer builder (not as an option property)
Output sets options.ServerSideSessions.RemoveExpiredSessionsFrequency to TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5)
Output sets options.ServerSideSessions.ExpiredSessionsTriggerBackchannelLogout to true
Output sets options.Authentication.CoordinateClientLifetimesWithUserSession to true

evals.prompt: Define a custom identity resource called 'organization' that includes the claims 'org_id', 'org_name', and 'org_role'. It should be marked as required so it's not shown as optional on the consent screen. Also define the standard openid and profile identity resources alongside it.
evals.expected_output: An IdentityResource collection containing IdentityResources.OpenId(), IdentityResources.Profile(), and a custom IdentityResource with name 'organization', the three specified user claims, and Required=true.
assertions: Output includes new IdentityResources.OpenId() in the identity resources collection
Output includes new IdentityResources.Profile() in the identity resources collection
Output defines a custom IdentityResource with name 'organization' and userClaims containing 'org_id', 'org_name', and 'org_role'
The custom 'organization' identity resource has Required set to true

evals.prompt: I'm deploying IdentityServer behind an Nginx reverse proxy at https://id.mycompany.com. Configure the IssuerUri correctly, set the authentication cookie lifetime to 8 hours with no sliding expiration, enable Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR) as required for all clients, and configure caching for client and resource stores with a 10-minute expiration.
evals.expected_output: IdentityServerOptions with IssuerUri set to the external URL, authentication cookie settings, PAR required, and caching configured.
assertions: Output sets options.IssuerUri to 'https://id.mycompany.com'
Output sets options.Authentication.CookieLifetime to TimeSpan.FromHours(8) and CookieSlidingExpiration to false
Output sets options.PushedAuthorization.Required to true
Output sets options.Caching.ClientStoreExpiration and options.Caching.ResourceStoreExpiration to TimeSpan.FromMinutes(10)

evals.prompt:
Review this client configuration and fix all the problems:

```csharp
new Client
{
    ClientId = "my-web-app",
    AllowedGrantTypes = GrantTypes.Code,
    ClientSecrets = { new Secret("super-secret-password") },
    RedirectUris = { "https://app.example.com/signin-oidc/" },
    AllowedScopes = { "profile", "email", "api1" },
    AllowOfflineAccess = false
}
```

The app needs to authenticate users, read their profile and email, call api1, and use refresh tokens. Explain each issue.
evals.expected_output: Identifies and fixes: (1) missing .Sha256() on the secret, (2) trailing slash mismatch on redirect URI, (3) missing 'openid' scope, (4) AllowOfflineAccess should be true for refresh tokens. Ideally also flags the hardcoded secret as a security concern.
assertions: Output identifies the missing .Sha256() call on the client secret and fixes it (e.g., new Secret("...".Sha256()))
Output identifies the trailing slash on the redirect URI as a potential mismatch issue and removes it
Output identifies the missing 'openid' scope (IdentityServerConstants.StandardScopes.OpenId) and adds it to AllowedScopes
Output changes AllowOfflineAccess to true to enable refresh token issuance
Output warns about hardcoding secrets in source code or recommends loading from configuration/vault

evals.prompt: I need to configure a machine-to-machine client called 'billing-service' for our payment processing backend. It needs access to two API scopes: 'invoices.read' and 'invoices.pay'. Show me the client definition and the API scope definitions. The secret should be loaded from configuration, not hardcoded.
evals.expected_output: A Client definition with GrantTypes.ClientCredentials, RequireClientSecret = true (or default), and AllowedScopes containing both scopes. Two ApiScope definitions. Secret loaded from configuration rather than hardcoded.
assertions: Client uses GrantTypes.ClientCredentials as the allowed grant type
Client AllowedScopes contains both 'invoices.read' and 'invoices.pay'
ApiScope definitions include 'invoices.read' and 'invoices.pay'
Secret is loaded from Configuration or an external source — not a hardcoded string with Sha256()

evals.prompt: I'm building a React SPA at https://spa.myapp.com that needs to authenticate users via our IdentityServer instance. The SPA needs to access user profile info and call our API at scope 'orders.read'. Configure the client properly. It also needs to be able to get refresh tokens.
evals.expected_output: A Client definition with GrantTypes.Code, RequireClientSecret = false (public client), RequirePkce = true, redirect URIs, CORS origins, AllowOfflineAccess = true, and appropriate scopes including openid, profile, and the API scope.
assertions: Uses GrantTypes.Code (not Implicit) as the grant type
Sets RequireClientSecret = false since it is a public SPA client
Includes AllowedCorsOrigins with 'https://spa.myapp.com'
Sets AllowOfflineAccess = true to enable refresh tokens
AllowedScopes includes 'openid', 'profile', and 'orders.read'

evals.prompt: We have two microservices: an Invoice API and a Customer API. They share some common scopes ('manage', 'enumerate') and each has their own specific scopes. I need the access tokens to include the correct 'aud' claim so each API can validate that the token is intended for it. Show me how to set up API Resources and API Scopes for this.
evals.expected_output: ApiScope definitions for service-specific scopes and shared scopes. Two ApiResource definitions ('invoice' and 'customer') that each reference their own scopes plus the shared scopes. Explanation that the aud claim is derived from the API Resource name.
assertions: Defines separate ApiScope instances for service-specific scopes (e.g. invoice.read, customer.read) and shared scopes (manage, enumerate)
Defines an ApiResource named 'invoice' with a Scopes collection referencing invoice-specific and shared scopes
Defines an ApiResource named 'customer' with a Scopes collection referencing customer-specific and shared scopes
Explains that the aud claim in the token is derived from the ApiResource Name property
Registers both API Resources and API Scopes with AddInMemoryApiResources and AddInMemoryApiScopes

evals.prompt: I want to define a custom identity resource called 'employee_info' that includes claims 'employee_id', 'department', and 'hire_date'. An interactive client should be able to request this scope. Show me the full configuration including the identity resource, client setup, and registration.
evals.expected_output: A custom IdentityResource with name 'employee_info' and UserClaims containing the three claim types. A client with 'employee_info' in its AllowedScopes alongside openid. Registration via AddInMemoryIdentityResources.
assertions: Creates a new IdentityResource with name 'employee_info'
The IdentityResource's UserClaims includes 'employee_id', 'department', and 'hire_date'
An interactive client includes 'employee_info' in its AllowedScopes along with 'openid'
The IdentityResources.OpenId() is still present (required by OIDC spec)

evals.prompt: I need to set up a confidential client that authenticates using private_key_jwt instead of a shared secret. The client has an X.509 certificate. Also enable JWT bearer client authentication on the server side. The client uses authorization code flow for a web app at https://webapp.example.com.
evals.expected_output: A Client definition with a Secret of type X509CertificateBase64 (or JsonWebKey), GrantTypes.Code, redirect URIs, and a call to AddJwtBearerClientAuthentication() on the IdentityServer builder.
assertions: Client secret uses Type = IdentityServerConstants.SecretTypes.X509CertificateBase64 or SecretTypes.JsonWebKey
Uses GrantTypes.Code as the grant type
Calls AddJwtBearerClientAuthentication() on the IdentityServer builder
Includes RedirectUris pointing to the web application
Does NOT use a shared secret (no Sha256() call on a string for this client)

evals.prompt: I need to configure refresh token behavior for our mobile app client. Requirements: refresh tokens should be one-time use (rotated on each use), have an absolute lifetime of 7 days, a sliding window of 1 day, and claims should be updated on each refresh. Show me the client configuration.
evals.expected_output: A Client definition with AllowOfflineAccess = true, RefreshTokenUsage = OneTimeOnly, AbsoluteRefreshTokenLifetime = 604800, SlidingRefreshTokenLifetime = 86400, RefreshTokenExpiration = Sliding, and UpdateAccessTokenClaimsOnRefresh = true.
assertions: Sets AllowOfflineAccess = true to enable refresh tokens
Sets RefreshTokenUsage to TokenUsage.OneTimeOnly for rotation on each use
Sets AbsoluteRefreshTokenLifetime to 604800 (7 days in seconds)
Sets SlidingRefreshTokenLifetime to 86400 (1 day in seconds) and RefreshTokenExpiration to TokenExpiration.Sliding
Sets UpdateAccessTokenClaimsOnRefresh = true

evals.prompt: I want to support zero-downtime secret rotation for our 'payment-gateway' client credentials client. It currently uses one shared secret but we need to add a new secret while keeping the old one valid during a transition period. Show me how to configure this and explain the rollover process.
evals.expected_output: A Client definition with multiple Secret entries in the ClientSecrets collection. Explanation that IdentityServer validates against all secrets, so the old one remains valid until removed.
assertions: Client has multiple Secret entries in the ClientSecrets collection (both old and new)
Uses GrantTypes.ClientCredentials for the client
Explains that IdentityServer validates against all configured secrets so both old and new work during rollover
Recommends removing the old secret after clients have migrated to the new one

evals.prompt: Our access tokens don't have an 'aud' claim and we need one for our single API. We don't want to set up full API Resources because we only have one API. What's the simplest way to get an audience claim in our tokens?
evals.expected_output: Enable EmitStaticAudienceClaim in IdentityServerOptions, which emits '{issuer}/resources' as the aud claim. Alternatively, define a minimal ApiResource.
assertions: Shows setting options.EmitStaticAudienceClaim = true in the IdentityServer options
Explains that this emits a static audience claim of '{issuer}/resources'
Mentions the alternative of defining an ApiResource as a more targeted approach
Does NOT suggest using the Implicit grant type or any deprecated patterns
